contract.goal: Port .claude/scripts/guild/derive-panel.ts (panel composer that parses .claude/agents/PANEL-COMPOSITION.md and emits comma-separated agent list) to .claude/cli/verbs/guild/derive-panel.ts. Byte-equivalent verb behavior. Spec path resolved against ctx.cwd. Stdin reads from ctx.stdin. loadSpec() signature changes to return {spec, warning} so the verb can surface fallback warnings via DispatchResult.stderr (vs the legacy script's eager process.stderr.write). D4 of 5 in Phase 2.
acceptance_criteria: derivePanelVerb(rest, ctx) exported. Args: --files=<csv> or stdin newline-separated. Spec at <ctx.cwd>/.claude/agents/PANEL-COMPOSITION.md.
Fallback to FALLBACK_RULES/FALLBACK_PRECEDENCE if parse fails; warning surfaces on stderr.
Pure helpers re-exported for unit testing.
Byte-parity confirmed via diff against legacy script.
rules_applied: loadSpec's new {spec, warning} return shape lets the verb populate DispatchResult.stderr cleanly without eager process.stderr writes.

actions: Wrote .claude/cli/verbs/guild/derive-panel.ts (~270 lines, ports legacy with loadSpec signature change to {spec, warning}).
Registered 'derive-panel' in GUILD_VERBS and VERBS help registry.
Wrote derive-panel.test.ts (31 tests: live-spec assertions + glob tests + verb tests + fallback tests + spec-parsing tests).
Verified parity via diff <(bin/guild derive-panel --files=...) <(node .claude/scripts/guild/derive-panel.ts --files=...) → exit 0 for representative inputs.

Discovered while testing: PANEL-COMPOSITION.md's `.claude/cli/**/*.ts` pattern requires `**` to match at least one segment, so files DIRECTLY in `.claude/cli/` (guild.ts, griot.ts) don't match this row and fall through to generic *.ts (which includes react-api). This is the cause of my Phase 1 D1-D4 manual-override rationale ('script's panel includes react-api') — the script is correct per the spec's literal pattern, the SPEC's pattern is too tight. Filing as separate substrate finding for the deferred sibling project; not D4's scope.
Ran npm test: 39 files / 554 tests pass.
Spawned evaluator-contract-fit + evaluator-naming in parallel. Both approved.

scope: Port derive-panel verb with byte-equivalent CLI output.
Refactor loadSpec to return {spec, warning} for verb-shape compatibility.
notes_for_pr: D4 of 5 in Phase 2. PR refresh at phase close.

loadSpec signature change is a deliberate verb-shape adaptation (returns {spec, warning} instead of just Spec, since DispatchResult can't do eager stderr writes mid-execution). Same observable behavior at the CLI layer — fallback warning appears on stderr.

Substrate finding discovered during D4: PANEL-COMPOSITION.md's `.claude/cli/**/*.ts` glob requires `**` to match at least one segment. So `.claude/cli/guild.ts` (directly in dir) doesn't match — falls through to generic `*.ts` rule (which adds react-api). My Phase 1 D1-D4 manual-override rationale was based on this misread; the script was correct per the spec's literal pattern. The SPEC's pattern is what needs adjusting (or the spec should add `| `.claude/cli/*.ts` | contract-fit, naming |` as a sibling row). Logged for the deferred sibling project.
